title: Master Course of Study — Universal Modeling Mastery
description: Complete textbook manifest for agentic generation of all required textbooks across the graduate modeling curriculum. Each entry contains the textbook title, metadata, and detailed chapter-by-chapter outlines with content descriptions sufficient to generate 15,000–40,000+ word textbooks per volume.

generation_instructions.style: Graduate-level textbook with rigorous mathematical exposition, worked examples, proofs where appropriate, and computational implementation notes in Julia/Python
generation_instructions.citations: Include references to primary literature (journal articles, foundational texts) with author-year format
generation_instructions.mathematical_notation: Use standard LaTeX-style notation descriptions; define all symbols on first use
generation_instructions.code_examples: Julia as primary language; Python where ecosystem is stronger; include runnable code blocks
generation_instructions.exercises: Include 5–10 exercises per chapter ranging from computational to proof-based

textbooks.title: Real Analysis for Modelers
textbooks.description: A rigorous treatment of real analysis with emphasis on the tools most essential for modeling: metric space theory, convergence, integration theory, and function spaces. Prepares the student for measure-theoretic probability and functional analysis.

chapters.title: The Real Number System and Completeness
chapters.content_outline: Construction of the reals via Dedekind cuts and Cauchy sequences. The completeness axiom (least upper bound property). Archimedean property. Density of rationals and irrationals. Countability and uncountability — Cantor's diagonal argument. Cardinality of the continuum. Why completeness matters for modeling: guarantees that limiting processes (iterative solvers, series expansions, optimization algorithms) converge to actual real numbers.

chapters.title: Metric Spaces: The Language of Proximity
chapters.content_outline: Definition and examples of metric spaces: Euclidean space, function spaces (sup norm, Lp norms), discrete metric, Hamming distance. Open and closed sets, interior, closure, boundary. Convergence of sequences in metric spaces. Cauchy sequences and completeness. The completion of a metric space. Equivalent metrics and topological equivalence. Product metrics. Applications: defining convergence of numerical methods, error metrics for model calibration.

chapters.title: Compactness
chapters.content_outline: Open covers and compactness. Sequential compactness. Heine-Borel theorem in Euclidean space. Bolzano-Weierstrass theorem. Compactness in general metric spaces — equivalence of sequential compactness and compactness in metric spaces. Total boundedness. Compact subsets of function spaces — Arzelà-Ascoli theorem. Applications: existence of optimal solutions in optimization, existence of maximizers of likelihood functions.

chapters.title: Connectedness and Continuity
chapters.content_outline: Connected and path-connected spaces. Intermediate value theorem as a consequence of connectedness. Continuous functions between metric spaces. Uniform continuity. Lipschitz continuity — connection to ODE existence theory (Picard-Lindelöf). Homeomorphisms and topological invariants. Continuous functions on compact sets: extreme value theorem, uniform continuity.

chapters.title: Sequences and Series of Functions
chapters.content_outline: Pointwise vs. uniform convergence. Weierstrass M-test. Interchange of limits: when can you swap limits with integrals, derivatives, or sums? Uniform convergence and continuity, integration, differentiation. Power series and radius of convergence. Stone-Weierstrass theorem — density of polynomials and its implications for approximation theory and machine learning (universal approximation).

chapters.title: Differentiation in Several Variables
chapters.content_outline: Total derivative as a linear map. Jacobian matrix. Chain rule for multivariable functions. Inverse function theorem and implicit function theorem — critical for sensitivity analysis and bifurcation theory. Higher-order derivatives, Taylor's theorem in several variables. Optimization: necessary and sufficient conditions, Hessian matrix, constrained optimization (Lagrange multipliers as a preview of variational methods).

chapters.title: Riemann Integration and Its Limitations
chapters.content_outline: Riemann integral via partitions, upper and lower sums. Riemann integrability criteria. Properties of the Riemann integral. Fundamental theorem of calculus. Improper integrals. Limitations of Riemann integration: non-integrability of the Dirichlet function, difficulties with pointwise limits of integrable functions. Motivation for Lebesgue integration — why modelers need a more powerful integral.

chapters.title: Introduction to Measure and Lebesgue Integration
chapters.content_outline: Sigma-algebras and measurable sets. Lebesgue outer measure and Lebesgue measure on the real line. Measurable functions. Lebesgue integral: simple functions, non-negative functions, general integrable functions. Monotone convergence theorem, Fatou's lemma, dominated convergence theorem — the three pillars that make Lebesgue integration powerful for analysis. Comparison with Riemann integration. Fubini's theorem preview.

chapters.title: Lp Spaces: A First Look at Function Spaces
chapters.content_outline: Definition of Lp spaces. Hölder's and Minkowski's inequalities. Completeness of Lp spaces (Riesz-Fischer theorem). L2 as a Hilbert space — inner product, orthogonality, Fourier series as orthogonal projections. Dense subsets: continuous functions are dense in Lp. Dual spaces: Riesz representation theorem for Lp. Why Lp spaces matter: PDE weak solutions live in Sobolev spaces built on Lp; probability densities are L1 functions; regression is projection in L2.

chapters.title: Fixed Point Theorems and Applications to Modeling
chapters.content_outline: Banach fixed point theorem (contraction mapping principle) — proof and applications. Existence and uniqueness of ODE solutions via Picard iteration. Brouwer and Schauder fixed point theorems. Applications: existence of Nash equilibria, existence of steady states in population models, convergence of iterative numerical methods (Jacobi, Gauss-Seidel). Fixed points as equilibria of dynamical systems.

textbooks.title: Linear Algebra and Matrix Theory for Computational Science
textbooks.description: Advanced linear algebra with emphasis on matrix decompositions, spectral theory, and computational aspects. Every model in the curriculum — from regression to finite elements to neural networks — is ultimately a problem in linear algebra.

chapters.title: Vector Spaces and Linear Maps
chapters.content_outline: Abstract vector spaces over real and complex fields. Subspaces, span, linear independence, basis, dimension. Linear maps: kernel, image, rank-nullity theorem. Matrix representations. Change of basis. Direct sums and projections. Quotient spaces. Dual spaces and the transpose.

chapters.title: Inner Product Spaces and Orthogonality
chapters.content_outline: Inner products on real and complex spaces. Cauchy-Schwarz and triangle inequalities. Orthogonal and orthonormal bases. Gram-Schmidt process. Orthogonal complements. Orthogonal projections — connection to least squares and best approximation. QR decomposition. Bessel's inequality and Parseval's identity.

chapters.title: Eigenvalues, Eigenvectors, and Diagonalization
chapters.content_outline: Characteristic polynomial. Algebraic and geometric multiplicity. Diagonalization criteria. Spectral theorem for real symmetric matrices and Hermitian matrices. Simultaneous diagonalization of commuting matrices. Spectral mapping theorem. Applications: stability of linear ODE systems (eigenvalues of the Jacobian), principal component analysis, normal modes of vibration.

chapters.title: The Singular Value Decomposition
chapters.content_outline: Existence and uniqueness of SVD. Geometric interpretation: SVD as a sequence of rotation-scaling-rotation. Truncated SVD and low-rank approximation — Eckart-Young theorem. Connection to PCA. Pseudoinverse via SVD. Numerical rank and condition number. Applications: image compression, dimensionality reduction, solving ill-conditioned linear systems, dynamic mode decomposition.

chapters.title: Matrix Decompositions for Computation
chapters.content_outline: LU decomposition with pivoting. Cholesky decomposition for positive definite matrices — connection to covariance matrices and Gaussian processes. Schur decomposition. Jordan canonical form (theoretical importance). QR algorithm for eigenvalue computation. Hessenberg reduction. Computational cost analysis: O(n³) for dense, importance of sparsity.

chapters.title: Norms, Condition Numbers, and Perturbation Theory
chapters.content_outline: Vector norms and matrix norms (operator, Frobenius). Equivalence of norms in finite dimensions. Condition number of a matrix — sensitivity of linear systems to perturbation. Perturbation bounds for eigenvalues (Weyl, Bauer-Fike). Perturbation of the SVD. Backward error analysis. Why condition numbers matter: numerical stability of every algorithm in scientific computing.

chapters.title: Iterative Methods for Large-Scale Linear Systems
chapters.content_outline: Motivation: when direct methods are too expensive (large sparse systems from PDEs). Jacobi, Gauss-Seidel, SOR methods and convergence criteria. Krylov subspace methods: conjugate gradient (for SPD systems), GMRES (for general systems), BiCGSTAB. Preconditioning: ILU, algebraic multigrid, domain decomposition. Convergence theory via spectral analysis.

chapters.title: Tensor Algebra and Multilinear Maps
chapters.content_outline: Tensors as multilinear maps. Index notation and Einstein summation convention. Tensor products of vector spaces. Symmetric and antisymmetric tensors. Tensor decompositions: CP decomposition, Tucker decomposition, tensor train. Applications: continuum mechanics (stress and strain tensors), higher-order statistics, tensor networks in physics and machine learning.

chapters.title: Nonnegative Matrix Factorization and Structured Matrices
chapters.content_outline: Nonnegative matrix factorization (NMF): algorithms and applications (topic modeling, spectral unmixing). Toeplitz and circulant matrices — connection to convolution and FFT. Sparse matrices: storage formats (CSR, CSC), graph representation. Band matrices from finite differences. Kronecker products and vec operator. Structured matrix computations in PDE solvers.

chapters.title: Matrix Functions and the Matrix Exponential
chapters.content_outline: Matrix functions via spectral decomposition, Jordan form, and Cauchy integral formula. The matrix exponential: definition, properties, computation (scaling and squaring, Padé approximants, Krylov methods). Application to linear ODE systems: x'(t) = Ax(t) has solution x(t) = exp(At)x(0). Connection to Lie groups and Lie algebras. Matrix logarithm. Applications: continuous-time Markov chains, linear stability analysis.

textbooks.title: Measure-Theoretic Probability I
textbooks.description: Rigorous probability theory built on measure theory. This is the mathematical foundation for all statistical inference, stochastic modeling, and uncertainty quantification in the curriculum.

chapters.title: Probability Spaces and σ-Algebras
chapters.content_outline: Sample spaces, events, σ-algebras. Why we need σ-algebras: non-measurable sets (Vitali). Borel σ-algebra on the real line. Probability measures: axioms, properties (continuity from above/below, inclusion-exclusion). Construction of probability measures via extension theorems (Carathéodory). Product σ-algebras and product measures.

chapters.title: Random Variables and Distribution Functions
chapters.content_outline: Random variables as measurable functions. Distribution functions: CDF properties, quantile functions. Discrete, continuous, and singular distributions. Lebesgue decomposition. Radon-Nikodym theorem — density functions as Radon-Nikodym derivatives. Random vectors, joint and marginal distributions. Borel measurability.

chapters.title: Expectation via the Lebesgue Integral
chapters.content_outline: Expectation defined as Lebesgue integral with respect to a probability measure. Properties: linearity, monotonicity, dominated convergence for expectations. Moments, variance, covariance, correlation. Moment generating functions and their properties. Jensen's inequality. Hölder and Minkowski inequalities for random variables. Lp spaces of random variables.

chapters.title: Independence and Product Measures
chapters.content_outline: Independence of events, σ-algebras, and random variables. Fubini-Tonelli theorem for product measures. Independent sequences: Kolmogorov 0-1 law. Kolmogorov's inequality. Construction of independent random variables on a common probability space. Grouping lemma. Convolution of distributions.

chapters.title: Modes of Convergence
chapters.content_outline: Almost sure convergence, convergence in probability, convergence in Lp, convergence in distribution. Relationships between modes: implications and counterexamples. Slutsky's theorem. Continuous mapping theorem. Borel-Cantelli lemmas (first and second). Convergence of random series — Kolmogorov three-series theorem.

chapters.title: Laws of Large Numbers
chapters.content_outline: Weak law of large numbers (Chebyshev's approach, Khintchine's theorem). Strong law of large numbers (Kolmogorov). Glivenko-Cantelli theorem — uniform convergence of empirical distribution functions. Applications: Monte Carlo estimation, bootstrap justification, consistency of estimators.

chapters.title: Characteristic Functions and the Central Limit Theorem
chapters.content_outline: Characteristic functions: definition, properties, inversion formula, uniqueness theorem. Lévy continuity theorem. Proof of the central limit theorem via characteristic functions. Berry-Esseen bound for rate of convergence. Multivariate CLT. Applications: asymptotic normality of MLEs, justification of normal approximations in biostatistics.

chapters.title: Conditional Expectation
chapters.content_outline: Conditional expectation given a σ-algebra: definition via Radon-Nikodym theorem. Properties: linearity, tower property (law of iterated expectations), conditional Jensen's inequality. Conditional expectation as L2 projection. Regular conditional distributions. Conditional independence. Importance for Bayesian inference: posterior expectation is conditional expectation given data.

chapters.title: Generating Functions, Transforms, and Inequalities
chapters.content_outline: Probability generating functions for discrete random variables. Moment generating functions and Laplace transforms. Chernoff bounds and sub-Gaussian random variables. Concentration inequalities: Hoeffding, Bernstein, McDiarmid. Sub-exponential random variables. Applications: high-dimensional statistics, learning theory, algorithm analysis.

chapters.title: Kolmogorov Extension and Infinite-Dimensional Probability
chapters.content_outline: Consistent families of finite-dimensional distributions. Kolmogorov extension theorem — constructing stochastic processes on infinite product spaces. Cylinder sets and the product σ-algebra. Application: rigorous construction of Brownian motion (Wiener measure). Gaussian processes defined via finite-dimensional distributions. Preview of stochastic processes.

textbooks.title: Scientific Computing I: Foundations of Numerical Methods
textbooks.description: Practical and theoretical foundations of numerical computation. Every model in the curriculum eventually requires numerical implementation; this course teaches the student to do it correctly, efficiently, and with rigorous error control.

chapters.title: Floating-Point Arithmetic and Error Analysis
chapters.content_outline: IEEE 754 standard. Machine epsilon, rounding modes, overflow/underflow. Catastrophic cancellation. Forward and backward error analysis. Conditioning of a problem vs. stability of an algorithm. Condition numbers revisited. Examples of numerical disasters: the Patriot missile failure, the Vancouver Stock Exchange. Julia/Python floating-point behavior.

chapters.title: Root Finding
chapters.content_outline: Bisection method: guaranteed convergence, linear rate. Newton's method: quadratic convergence, convergence basin, connection to fixed-point iteration. Secant method. Brent's method — combining reliability and speed. Systems of nonlinear equations: multivariate Newton. Convergence theory: contractive mappings. Applications: finding equilibria of ODE models, solving implicit numerical schemes.

chapters.title: Polynomial Interpolation and Approximation
chapters.content_outline: Lagrange and Newton interpolation. Runge phenomenon and the danger of high-degree polynomials. Chebyshev nodes and near-optimal interpolation. Piecewise polynomial interpolation: linear, cubic splines (natural, clamped, not-a-knot). B-splines and NURBS. Approximation theory: Weierstrass theorem, best approximation, minimax. Connection to finite element basis functions.

chapters.title: Numerical Differentiation and Integration
chapters.content_outline: Finite difference formulas: forward, backward, central. Truncation error analysis via Taylor expansion. Richardson extrapolation. Automatic differentiation (forward and reverse mode) — the computational backbone of modern ML and SciML. Numerical quadrature: Newton-Cotes (trapezoid, Simpson), Gaussian quadrature, adaptive quadrature. Monte Carlo integration for high dimensions. Sparse grids.

chapters.title: Direct Methods for Linear Systems
chapters.content_outline: Gaussian elimination with partial pivoting. LU decomposition: existence, uniqueness for nonsingular matrices, computational cost O(n³). Cholesky for symmetric positive definite systems: half the cost. Banded systems: O(n) for tridiagonal. QR factorization via Householder reflectors. Least squares via QR and SVD. Sparse direct solvers. Julia LinearAlgebra and SparseArrays packages.

chapters.title: Iterative Methods for Linear Systems
chapters.content_outline: Classical iterations: Jacobi, Gauss-Seidel, SOR. Convergence conditions via spectral radius. Krylov subspace methods: conjugate gradient method for SPD systems — derivation, convergence, preconditioning. GMRES for non-symmetric systems. Preconditioning strategies: incomplete factorizations, multigrid preview. When to use iterative vs. direct: problem size and sparsity considerations.

chapters.title: Eigenvalue Problems
chapters.content_outline: Power method and inverse iteration. QR algorithm: shifts, implicit QR, Hessenberg reduction. Symmetric eigenvalue problems: Jacobi rotations, divide-and-conquer, MRRR. SVD computation. Eigenvalue problems in modeling: stability analysis (computing eigenvalues of large Jacobians), PCA (eigenvalues of covariance matrices), Google PageRank. Julia eigen() and svd() functions.

chapters.title: Numerical Methods for Initial Value ODEs
chapters.content_outline: Euler's method: forward and backward. Runge-Kutta methods: derivation, order conditions, embedded pairs (Dormand-Prince). Multistep methods: Adams-Bashforth, Adams-Moulton, BDF. Stiffness: what it is, why explicit methods fail, A-stability and L-stability. Adaptive time-stepping: error estimation and step-size control. Julia DifferentialEquations.jl: solving ODE systems with automatic stiffness detection.

chapters.title: Fast Algorithms: FFT, Fast Multipole, and Randomized Methods
chapters.content_outline: Discrete Fourier transform and the Cooley-Tukey FFT algorithm. Applications: spectral methods for PDEs, convolution, signal processing. Fast multipole method for N-body problems. Randomized algorithms: randomized SVD, random projection, sketching. Complexity reduction from O(n²) or O(n³) to O(n log n) or O(n). When to use randomized methods: large-scale data and simulation.

chapters.title: Software Engineering for Scientific Computing
chapters.content_outline: Version control with Git for computational research. Reproducibility: random seeds, environment management (Julia Project.toml, Python requirements.txt). Testing scientific code: unit tests, convergence tests, method of manufactured solutions. Profiling and optimization: identifying bottlenecks, memory allocation, type stability in Julia. Parallelism: multithreading, distributed computing, GPU computing basics. Documentation and literate programming (Quarto, Pluto notebooks).

textbooks.title: Functional Analysis for Modelers
textbooks.description: The theory of infinite-dimensional vector spaces and operators. Functional analysis provides the rigorous framework for PDEs, integral equations, optimization in function spaces, and the mathematical foundations of machine learning (reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces, universal approximation).

chapters.title: Banach Spaces
chapters.content_outline: Normed spaces and Banach spaces. Examples: C[a,b], Lp spaces, sequence spaces (ℓp). Completion of normed spaces. Schauder bases. Separability. Finite-dimensional vs. infinite-dimensional: compactness of the unit ball characterizes finite dimension. Applications: why PDE solutions live in Banach spaces.

chapters.title: Hilbert Spaces
chapters.content_outline: Inner product spaces and Hilbert spaces. Orthogonal complements and the projection theorem. Riesz representation theorem. Orthonormal bases and Parseval's identity. Fourier series as expansion in orthonormal basis of L2. Separable Hilbert spaces are all isomorphic to ℓ2. Reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces (RKHS) — connection to Gaussian processes and kernel methods in machine learning.

chapters.title: Bounded Linear Operators
chapters.content_outline: Bounded linear operators between Banach spaces. Operator norm. Space of bounded operators B(X,Y). Uniform boundedness principle (Banach-Steinhaus). Open mapping theorem. Closed graph theorem. Applications: proving continuity of solution operators for PDEs, well-posedness of inverse problems.

chapters.title: The Hahn-Banach Theorem and Duality
chapters.content_outline: Hahn-Banach theorem (analytic and geometric forms). Dual spaces. Reflexive spaces. Weak and weak* topologies. Banach-Alaoglu theorem: weak* compactness of the unit ball. Applications: existence of solutions to optimization problems via weak compactness, duality in convex optimization.

chapters.title: Compact Operators and Spectral Theory
chapters.content_outline: Compact operators: definition, properties, examples (integral operators, embedding operators). Spectral theorem for compact self-adjoint operators. Fredholm alternative. Hilbert-Schmidt operators. Trace class operators. Mercer's theorem — connection to kernel methods and Gaussian processes. Applications: spectral methods for PDEs, Karhunen-Loève expansion for random fields.

chapters.title: Sobolev Spaces
chapters.content_outline: Weak derivatives. Sobolev spaces Wk,p and Hk = Wk,2. Sobolev embedding theorems. Trace theorems: restricting Sobolev functions to boundaries. Poincaré and Friedrichs inequalities. Rellich-Kondrachov compactness theorem. Why Sobolev spaces matter: PDE weak solutions, finite element error analysis, regularity theory.

chapters.title: Variational Formulations and Weak Solutions
chapters.content_outline: Bilinear forms: continuity and coercivity. Lax-Milgram theorem — existence and uniqueness for elliptic PDEs in weak form. Galerkin approximation: projecting onto finite-dimensional subspaces. Céa's lemma: quasi-optimality of Galerkin solutions. Connection to finite element methods. Variational inequalities. Saddle point problems (Stokes equations, mixed methods).

chapters.title: Semigroups of Operators
chapters.content_outline: Strongly continuous semigroups (C0-semigroups). Generators: Hille-Yosida theorem. Lumer-Phillips theorem for dissipative operators. Examples: heat semigroup, translation semigroup, Markov semigroups. Application to evolution equations: existence of solutions to ut = Au via semigroup theory. Analytic semigroups and parabolic equations. Connection to operator splitting methods.

chapters.title: Distribution Theory
chapters.content_outline: Test functions and distributions (generalized functions). Dirac delta as a distribution. Derivatives of distributions. Fundamental solutions: Green's functions as distributional solutions. Fourier transform of distributions. Applications: singular source terms in PDEs, point masses in probability, impulse forcing in mechanics.

chapters.title: Approximation Theory and Universal Approximation
chapters.content_outline: Best approximation in normed spaces. Projection theorems. Kolmogorov n-widths: optimal approximation rates. Neural network universal approximation theorems (Cybenko, Hornik, Barron). Approximation rates for neural networks vs. polynomials vs. wavelets. Curse of dimensionality and how neural networks circumvent it (Barron spaces). Connection to SciML: neural networks as function approximators in differential equation models.

textbooks.title: Probability Theory II: Martingales, Brownian Motion, and Stochastic Calculus Foundations
textbooks.description: Advanced probability covering martingale theory, Brownian motion, and the foundations of stochastic calculus. Essential for stochastic modeling in every domain track.

chapters.title: Conditional Expectation: Advanced Properties
chapters.content_outline: Review of conditional expectation as L2 projection. Conditional expectation with respect to increasing σ-algebras (filtrations). Regular conditional probabilities. Disintegration theorem. Conditional independence and graphical models preview. Tower property as a computational tool.

chapters.title: Martingale Theory
chapters.content_outline: Filtrations and adapted processes. Martingales, submartingales, supermartingales. Examples: random walks, Pólya urns, Doob martingales. Doob's decomposition. Optional stopping theorem — conditions and counterexamples. Martingale convergence theorems. Doob's maximal inequality. Azuma-Hoeffding inequality. Applications: sequential analysis, optional stopping in clinical trials, convergence of stochastic algorithms.

chapters.title: Construction of Brownian Motion
chapters.content_outline: Definition and properties of Brownian motion. Lévy's construction. Nowhere differentiability of Brownian paths (a.s.). Quadratic variation. Brownian motion as a Gaussian process. Markov property and strong Markov property. Reflection principle. Maximum and hitting times. Brownian motion as the scaling limit of random walks (Donsker's theorem).

chapters.title: Continuous-Time Martingales and Stochastic Integration Preview
chapters.content_outline: Continuous-time filtrations and adapted processes. Brownian motion as a continuous-time martingale. Quadratic variation process. Lévy's characterization of Brownian motion. Stochastic integral of simple processes with respect to Brownian motion. Extension to L2 integrand processes. Itô isometry. Preview of Itô's formula.

chapters.title: Central Limit Theorem: Extensions and Refinements
chapters.content_outline: Lindeberg-Feller CLT for triangular arrays. Lyapunov condition. CLT for martingale difference sequences. Multivariate CLT. Delta method. Edgeworth expansions. CLT for dependent sequences: mixing conditions. Functional CLT (Donsker's invariance principle). Applications: asymptotic inference, diffusion approximations of discrete models.

chapters.title: Convergence of Random Series and Tail Events
chapters.content_outline: Kolmogorov three-series theorem. Hewitt-Savage 0-1 law. Tail σ-algebra and tail events. Convergence of random power series. Kolmogorov's maximal inequality. Strong law via martingale methods. Application: almost sure convergence of MCMC algorithms, convergence of stochastic gradient descent.

chapters.title: Weak Convergence and Empirical Processes
chapters.content_outline: Weak convergence of probability measures. Portmanteau theorem. Prokhorov's theorem: tightness and relative compactness. Skorokhod's representation theorem. Empirical processes: Glivenko-Cantelli and Donsker theorems. Quantile processes. Applications: bootstrap theory, goodness-of-fit tests, nonparametric inference.

chapters.title: Large Deviations
chapters.content_outline: Large deviation principle. Cramér's theorem. Rate functions and exponential tightness. Varadhan's integral lemma. Sanov's theorem for empirical measures. Gärtner-Ellis theorem. Applications: rare event simulation, importance sampling design, asymptotic efficiency of hypothesis tests, statistical mechanics connections.

textbooks.title: Ordinary Differential Equations: Theory, Analysis, and Computation
textbooks.description: ODEs are the language of dynamics. This course covers existence theory, qualitative analysis, stability, and numerical methods — preparing the student for every dynamical model in the curriculum.

chapters.title: Existence and Uniqueness
chapters.content_outline: Initial value problems. Picard-Lindelöf theorem: existence and uniqueness via contraction mapping. Peano existence theorem (existence without Lipschitz). Maximal interval of existence. Gronwall's inequality. Continuous dependence on initial conditions and parameters. Sensitivity equations. Extension and blow-up.

chapters.title: Linear Systems
chapters.content_outline: Homogeneous linear systems: fundamental matrix, matrix exponential. Variation of parameters. Constant coefficient systems: eigenvalue classification (nodes, spirals, saddles, centers). Jordan normal form solutions. Floquet theory for periodic coefficients. Linear stability and the spectral abscissa.

chapters.title: Phase Plane Analysis and Qualitative Theory
chapters.content_outline: Phase portraits for 2D systems. Classification of equilibria via linearization. Hartman-Grobman theorem. Poincaré-Bendixson theorem: limit cycles in 2D. Dulac's criterion. Index theory. Nullclines and trapping regions. Applications: predator-prey models, SIR epidemics, Fitzhugh-Nagumo neuron model.

chapters.title: Stability Theory
chapters.content_outline: Lyapunov stability, asymptotic stability, exponential stability. Lyapunov's direct method: constructing Lyapunov functions. LaSalle invariance principle. Linearization and Lyapunov's indirect method. Input-to-state stability. Applications: proving stability of ecological equilibria, control system design, convergence of optimization algorithms.

chapters.title: Bifurcation Theory
chapters.content_outline: Structural stability and bifurcations. Saddle-node bifurcation. Transcritical and pitchfork bifurcations. Hopf bifurcation: birth of limit cycles. Center manifold reduction. Normal forms. Codimension-one bifurcations. Bifurcation diagrams. Numerical continuation (AUTO, BifurcationKit.jl). Applications: tipping points in ecological models, onset of oscillations in physiological systems.

chapters.title: Perturbation Methods
chapters.content_outline: Regular perturbation expansions. Singular perturbations: boundary layers, matched asymptotic expansions. Multiple scales method. WKB approximation. Averaging methods for oscillatory systems. Poincaré-Lindstedt method. Applications: slow-fast dynamics in neuroscience, geophysical flows, pharmacokinetics.

chapters.title: Delay Differential Equations
chapters.content_outline: DDEs: existence, uniqueness, infinite-dimensional phase space. Characteristic equations with exponential terms. Stability of DDEs: D-subdivisions, characteristic root methods. Hopf bifurcation in DDEs. Numerical methods for DDEs (method of steps, continuous Runge-Kutta). Applications: population dynamics with maturation delays, immune response with delay, neural networks with transmission delay.

chapters.title: Numerical Methods for ODE Systems
chapters.content_outline: Comprehensive treatment: explicit Runge-Kutta (ERK), implicit Runge-Kutta (SDIRK, Radau), BDF methods, Adams methods. Error estimation and adaptive stepping. Stiffness detection. Symplectic integrators for Hamiltonian systems (Störmer-Verlet, symplectic Euler). Exponential integrators for semilinear problems. Event handling: zero-crossing detection for discontinuities. DifferentialEquations.jl ecosystem.

textbooks.title: Mathematical Statistics: Inference, Estimation, and Testing
textbooks.description: The rigorous theory of statistical inference. Provides the mathematical foundations for every data-facing component of the curriculum: parameter estimation, hypothesis testing, model selection, and the asymptotic theory that justifies practical methods.

chapters.title: Statistical Models and Sufficiency
chapters.content_outline: Statistical models as parametric families of distributions. The likelihood function. Sufficient statistics: Fisher-Neyman factorization theorem. Minimal sufficiency. Complete statistics. Ancillary statistics. Basu's theorem: independence of complete sufficient and ancillary statistics. Exponential families: canonical form, sufficient statistics, moment/cumulant generation.

chapters.title: Point Estimation Theory
chapters.content_outline: Method of moments. Maximum likelihood estimation: consistency, asymptotic normality, asymptotic efficiency (achieving Cramér-Rao bound). Fisher information and its geometric interpretation. UMVUE: Rao-Blackwell theorem, Lehmann-Scheffé theorem. Equivariant estimators. James-Stein estimator and the inadmissibility of MLE in dimension ≥ 3. Shrinkage estimation.

chapters.title: Hypothesis Testing
chapters.content_outline: Neyman-Pearson lemma: most powerful tests for simple hypotheses. Uniformly most powerful tests. Monotone likelihood ratio. Likelihood ratio tests: Wilks' theorem (asymptotic chi-squared distribution). Score tests and Wald tests. Multiple testing: Bonferroni, Holm, Benjamini-Hochberg FDR control. P-values: exact vs. asymptotic, misinterpretation issues.

chapters.title: Confidence Regions and Interval Estimation
chapters.content_outline: Pivotal quantities. Exact confidence intervals for normal populations. Asymptotic confidence intervals via MLE theory. Profile likelihood intervals. Bootstrap confidence intervals: percentile, BCa, studentized. Coverage probability and expected length. Simultaneous confidence intervals. Bayesian credible intervals vs. frequentist confidence intervals.

chapters.title: Asymptotic Theory
chapters.content_outline: Consistency: conditions and proofs. Asymptotic normality of MLEs: regularity conditions, proof via Taylor expansion of the score function. Asymptotic relative efficiency. Contiguity and Le Cam's theory. Local asymptotic normality (LAN). Semiparametric efficiency bounds. Delta method and variance stabilizing transformations. Applications: justifying large-sample inference in biostatistics.

chapters.title: Decision Theory
chapters.content_outline: Statistical decision theory: loss functions, risk functions, Bayes risk, minimax risk. Admissibility and completeness. Bayes procedures as admissible procedures. Minimax estimation. James-Stein as empirical Bayes. Connection to machine learning: empirical risk minimization, structural risk minimization. PAC learning bounds.

chapters.title: Nonparametric and Robust Methods
chapters.content_outline: Empirical distribution function and plug-in principle. U-statistics: Hoeffding decomposition, asymptotic normality. Kernel density estimation: bandwidth selection, bias-variance tradeoff. Nonparametric regression: local polynomial, kernel smoothing, splines. Rank-based tests: Wilcoxon, Kruskal-Wallis. Robust estimation: M-estimators, influence functions, breakdown point. Quantile regression.

chapters.title: Information-Theoretic Model Selection
chapters.content_outline: Kullback-Leibler divergence as the foundational model selection criterion. AIC: derivation from KL divergence, bias correction, AICc. BIC: Bayesian justification, consistency. Cross-validation: leave-one-out, K-fold, generalized CV. Minimum description length principle. Focused information criterion (FIC). Model averaging. Applications: choosing among competing models in any domain.

textbooks.title: Partial Differential Equations I: Classical and Weak Solutions
textbooks.description: PDEs are the mathematical language of spatially extended systems: diffusion, wave propagation, fluid flow, electrostatics, population spread, and more. This course covers classical theory, weak solutions, and variational methods.

chapters.title: Classification and Canonical Forms
chapters.content_outline: First-order PDEs: method of characteristics, transport equation. Classification of second-order linear PDEs: elliptic (Laplace, Poisson), parabolic (heat equation), hyperbolic (wave equation). Characteristics and well-posedness. Physical interpretation of each type. Systems of PDEs: classification via eigenvalues of symbol matrix.

chapters.title: The Laplace and Poisson Equations
chapters.content_outline: Fundamental solution. Mean value property. Maximum principle (weak and strong). Regularity of harmonic functions. Dirichlet and Neumann boundary conditions. Green's functions for standard domains. Poisson's formula. Boundary regularity. Harnack inequality. Perron's method for existence. Applications: steady-state heat conduction, electrostatics, potential theory in geophysics.

chapters.title: The Heat Equation
chapters.content_outline: Derivation from conservation of energy. Fundamental solution (heat kernel). Maximum principle for parabolic equations. Uniqueness and stability. Duhamel's principle for inhomogeneous problems. Separation of variables and Fourier series solutions. Long-time behavior and approach to equilibrium. Energy methods. Applications: diffusion in tissue, population spread, option pricing (Black-Scholes as a heat equation).

chapters.title: The Wave Equation
chapters.content_outline: D'Alembert's formula in 1D. Kirchhoff's formula in 3D. Hadamard's method of descent. Huygens' principle: why you hear sharp sounds in 3D but not in 2D. Energy conservation. Domain of dependence and finite propagation speed. Reflection and transmission at interfaces. Applications: seismic waves, acoustic propagation, elastic wave propagation in biomechanics.

chapters.title: Fourier Analysis Methods for PDEs
chapters.content_outline: Fourier series solutions for boundary value problems. Fourier transform and its application to PDEs on unbounded domains. Spectral representation of differential operators. Parseval's theorem and energy in frequency domain. Discrete Fourier transform and aliasing. Applications: spectral methods for numerical solution, signal processing interpretation of PDE solutions.

chapters.title: Green's Functions and Integral Representations
chapters.content_outline: Green's functions for boundary value problems. Existence via Fredholm theory. Symmetry of Green's functions. Construction methods: images, eigenfunction expansion, conformal mapping. Boundary integral equations. Applications: BEM (boundary element method), electrostatics, potential flow, geophysical inverse problems.

chapters.title: Weak Solutions and Sobolev Spaces
chapters.content_outline: Motivation: classical solutions may not exist or may be insufficient. Weak formulation of elliptic problems. Lax-Milgram theorem for existence and uniqueness. Sobolev embedding and regularity. Weak solutions of parabolic and hyperbolic equations. Lions-Magenes theory. Galerkin approximations and convergence. Connection to finite element methods.

chapters.title: Variational Methods and Calculus of Variations
chapters.content_outline: Euler-Lagrange equations. Direct method of calculus of variations: existence via lower semicontinuity and coercivity. Regularity of minimizers. Constraints and Lagrange multipliers in infinite dimensions. Rayleigh quotient and eigenvalue problems. Variational principles in mechanics: Hamilton's principle, principle of virtual work. Connection to optimization.

chapters.title: Introduction to Nonlinear PDEs
chapters.content_outline: Nonlinear elliptic equations: monotone operator theory, fixed-point methods. Semilinear heat equation: blow-up in finite time. Reaction-diffusion equations: traveling wave solutions, Fisher-KPP equation. Conservation laws: Burgers equation, shock formation, Rankine-Hugoniot conditions, entropy conditions. Viscosity solutions of Hamilton-Jacobi equations.

chapters.title: Eigenvalue Problems for PDEs
chapters.content_outline: Sturm-Liouville theory. Spectral theorem for elliptic operators. Eigenfunction expansions. Rayleigh-Ritz variational characterization of eigenvalues. Asymptotic distribution of eigenvalues (Weyl's law). Spectral gaps and stability. Applications: vibration modes (structural, acoustic), quantum mechanics, stability of steady states in reaction-diffusion.

textbooks.title: Stochastic Processes for Modeling
textbooks.description: Stochastic processes provide the mathematical framework for modeling randomness that evolves over time. This course covers the major process types used across all domain tracks.

chapters.title: Discrete-Time Markov Chains
chapters.content_outline: Transition matrices, Chapman-Kolmogorov equations. Classification of states: transient, recurrent, absorbing. Stationary distributions: existence, uniqueness, convergence. Ergodic theorem. First passage times. Absorbing Markov chains. Applications: disease progression models, population genetics (Wright-Fisher model), MCMC algorithms.

chapters.title: Continuous-Time Markov Chains
chapters.content_outline: Generator matrix (Q-matrix). Kolmogorov forward and backward equations. Embedded discrete-time chain. Birth-death processes. Poisson process as a special case. Explosion and regularity. Stationary distributions and detailed balance. Reversible chains. Applications: chemical kinetics (Gillespie algorithm), queueing theory (M/M/1, M/M/c), compartmental models in continuous time.

chapters.title: Poisson Processes and Generalizations
chapters.content_outline: Homogeneous Poisson process: construction, properties, inter-arrival times. Superposition and thinning. Non-homogeneous Poisson processes. Compound Poisson processes. Poisson random measures. Spatial Poisson processes. Cox processes (doubly stochastic). Applications: event modeling (disease outbreaks, earthquakes), spatial point patterns, insurance claims.

chapters.title: Renewal Theory
chapters.content_outline: Renewal processes and the renewal equation. Elementary renewal theorem. Key renewal theorem. Renewal reward theorem. Alternating renewal processes. Regenerative processes. Applications: replacement and maintenance models, age-dependent branching processes, semi-Markov processes.

chapters.title: Branching Processes
chapters.content_outline: Galton-Watson branching processes: extinction probability, generating function methods. Subcritical, critical, supercritical classification. Multi-type branching processes. Age-dependent (Bellman-Harris) branching processes. Branching processes in random environments. Applications: extinction of populations, spread of epidemics in early phase, cell division, cascade failures.

chapters.title: Brownian Motion and Diffusion Processes
chapters.content_outline: Review of Brownian motion properties. Brownian motion with drift. Geometric Brownian motion. Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process. Diffusion processes: infinitesimal generator, Kolmogorov forward (Fokker-Planck) and backward equations. Scale functions and speed measures. Boundary classification (Feller). First passage times for diffusions. Applications: population diffusion, financial modeling, neural firing models.

chapters.title: Stochastic Differential Equations
chapters.content_outline: Itô integral: construction, properties, Itô isometry. Itô's formula. Stochastic differential equations: existence and uniqueness (Lipschitz conditions). Stratonovich integral and its relation to Itô. Geometric Brownian motion SDE. Ornstein-Uhlenbeck SDE. Numerical methods: Euler-Maruyama, Milstein, strong vs. weak convergence. Julia DifferentialEquations.jl SDE solvers.

chapters.title: Markov Random Fields and Spatial Stochastic Processes
chapters.content_outline: Gibbs distributions and Markov random fields. Hammersley-Clifford theorem. Ising model and Potts model. Gaussian Markov random fields (GMRFs): precision matrices, sparse structure. Connection between GPs and GMRFs via SPDE approach. Simulation: Gibbs sampler for MRFs. Applications: spatial statistics, image analysis, disease mapping (BYM model).

textbooks.title: Bayesian Statistical Theory and Computation
textbooks.description: Bayesian inference is the universal framework for learning from data under uncertainty. This course covers the full theory from prior specification through posterior computation and model comparison.

chapters.title: Foundations of Bayesian Inference
chapters.content_outline: Bayes' theorem as an updating rule. Prior, likelihood, posterior. Subjective vs. objective Bayes. De Finetti's theorem: exchangeability implies mixture of iid. Bayesian vs. frequentist: philosophical and practical contrasts. Bayesian decision theory: posterior expected loss, Bayes estimators. Connection to regularization.

chapters.title: Prior Specification
chapters.content_outline: Conjugate priors: beta-binomial, normal-normal, gamma-Poisson. Jeffreys priors and reference priors. Maximum entropy priors. Weakly informative priors (Gelman's recommendations). Penalized complexity priors. Prior predictive checking. Prior sensitivity analysis. Hierarchical priors: hyperpriors and partial pooling. Improper priors and their dangers.

chapters.title: Conjugate and Analytical Solutions
chapters.content_outline: Complete analytical solutions for exponential family models. Normal-normal, binomial-beta, Poisson-gamma, multinomial-Dirichlet, normal-inverse-gamma. Predictive distributions. Sufficiency and Bayesian updating. Natural conjugate families. Limitations of conjugate analysis: when models are not in exponential family.

chapters.title: Markov Chain Monte Carlo: Theory
chapters.content_outline: Motivation: posterior distributions we cannot compute analytically. Metropolis-Hastings algorithm: detailed balance, ergodicity, convergence. Gibbs sampler as a special case. Random-walk Metropolis: acceptance rates, scaling theory (Roberts-Rosenthal). Convergence diagnostics: R-hat, ESS, trace plots, autocorrelation. Burn-in and thinning. Geometric ergodicity and CLT for MCMC.

chapters.title: Hamiltonian Monte Carlo and Modern Samplers
chapters.content_outline: Hamiltonian dynamics and the leapfrog integrator. HMC: volume preservation, time reversibility, detailed balance. NUTS (No-U-Turn Sampler): adaptive trajectory length. Mass matrix adaptation. Comparison with random-walk MH: exploration efficiency in high dimensions. Riemannian HMC for challenging geometries. Practical implementation in Stan, Turing.jl, NumPyro.

chapters.title: Variational Inference
chapters.content_outline: Evidence lower bound (ELBO). Mean-field variational family. Coordinate ascent variational inference (CAVI). Stochastic variational inference (SVI) for large datasets. Black-box variational inference: reparameterization trick, score function estimator. Normalizing flows for flexible variational families. Comparison with MCMC: speed vs. accuracy tradeoff. Pathologies of variational inference.

chapters.title: Hierarchical and Multilevel Models
chapters.content_outline: Partial pooling: shrinkage toward group means. Random intercepts and random slopes. Non-centered parameterization for efficient MCMC. Hierarchical priors for variance components. Meta-analysis as hierarchical modeling. Spatial hierarchical models. Exchangeability and its generalizations. Applications: clinical trial meta-analysis, school effects, spatial disease mapping.

chapters.title: Model Comparison and Selection
chapters.content_outline: Marginal likelihood (model evidence). Bayes factors: computation, interpretation, Jeffreys' scale. Challenges of Bayes factor computation: bridge sampling, nested sampling, thermodynamic integration. Information criteria: DIC, WAIC, LOO-CV (Pareto-smoothed importance sampling). Bayesian model averaging. Bayesian stacking. Posterior predictive checking: replicated data, test quantities.

chapters.title: Bayesian Nonparametrics
chapters.content_outline: Dirichlet process: stick-breaking construction, Chinese restaurant process, Pólya urn. Dirichlet process mixture models. Gaussian process regression: kernel selection, hyperparameter optimization, sparse approximations. Beta process and Indian buffet process. Bayesian nonparametric survival analysis. Infinite-dimensional priors. Applications: density estimation, clustering, flexible regression.

chapters.title: Approximate Bayesian Computation and Simulation-Based Inference
chapters.content_outline: Likelihood-free inference: when the likelihood is intractable. ABC rejection, ABC-MCMC, ABC-SMC. Choice of summary statistics. Regression adjustment. Neural density estimation: learning posterior or likelihood with neural networks. Simulation-based inference (SBI) frameworks. Applications: agent-based model calibration, evolutionary models, complex mechanistic models in ecology and epidemiology.

textbooks.title: Numerical Methods for Differential Equations
textbooks.description: Rigorous numerical methods for solving the differential equations that arise in every domain of this curriculum. Covers finite difference, finite element, and spectral methods with convergence theory.

chapters.title: Finite Difference Methods for Elliptic Problems
chapters.content_outline: Grid generation. Central difference approximation of the Laplacian. Consistency, stability, and convergence. Maximum principle for discrete Laplacian. Error estimates. Irregular domains: cut-cell methods, immersed boundary. Multigrid methods: smoothing, restriction, prolongation, V-cycle, W-cycle, convergence theory.

chapters.title: Finite Difference Methods for Parabolic Problems
chapters.content_outline: Forward Euler (explicit), backward Euler (implicit), Crank-Nicolson. Von Neumann stability analysis. CFL condition. Lax equivalence theorem: consistency + stability = convergence. Method of lines: semi-discretize in space, use ODE solver in time. ADI (alternating direction implicit) for 2D problems. Applications: heat conduction, diffusion, Black-Scholes.

chapters.title: Finite Difference Methods for Hyperbolic Problems
chapters.content_outline: Upwind scheme for advection. Lax-Friedrichs and Lax-Wendroff schemes. Numerical dispersion and dissipation. CFL condition for hyperbolic equations. TVD (total variation diminishing) schemes. ENO and WENO schemes for shock capturing. Godunov's method and Riemann solvers. Applications: wave propagation, conservation laws, shallow water equations.

chapters.title: Finite Element Methods: Theory
chapters.content_outline: Weak formulation and Galerkin approximation. Finite element spaces: piecewise linear, quadratic, higher-order. Céa's lemma and best approximation. A priori error estimates in H1 and L2 norms. Mesh generation: triangulation, quality measures. Isoparametric elements. Implementation: assembly of stiffness matrix and load vector. Quadrature for element integrals.

chapters.title: Finite Element Methods: Advanced Topics
chapters.content_outline: Mixed methods for saddle-point problems (Stokes, elasticity). Inf-sup (LBB) condition. A posteriori error estimation: residual-based, recovery-based (Zienkiewicz-Zhu). Adaptive mesh refinement. Discontinuous Galerkin methods. Stabilized methods: SUPG, GLS. Nonlinear problems: Newton's method on the discrete system. Time-dependent problems: semi-discrete and fully discrete schemes.

chapters.title: Spectral Methods
chapters.content_outline: Fourier spectral methods for periodic problems. Chebyshev spectral methods for non-periodic problems. Spectral convergence: exponential vs. algebraic rates. Galerkin vs. collocation (pseudospectral) methods. Spectral element methods: combining spectral accuracy with geometric flexibility. Applications: turbulence simulation, atmospheric modeling (spherical harmonics), high-accuracy solutions.

chapters.title: Finite Volume Methods
chapters.content_outline: Conservation form and cell-averaged quantities. Flux computation: central, upwind, Riemann-solver based. Reconstruction: piecewise constant (first order), piecewise linear with limiters (second order), WENO (high order). Structured and unstructured grids. Applications: computational fluid dynamics, shallow water equations, advection-dominated transport.

chapters.title: Structure-Preserving and Geometric Integrators
chapters.content_outline: Symplectic integrators for Hamiltonian systems: symplectic Euler, Störmer-Verlet, Gauss-Legendre Runge-Kutta. Long-time energy conservation. Lie group integrators for systems on manifolds. Discrete exterior calculus and compatible discretizations. Positivity-preserving schemes. Mass-conserving schemes. Applications: molecular dynamics, celestial mechanics, incompressible flow, population models (positivity).

textbooks.title: Differential Geometry and Tensor Analysis for Physical Modeling
textbooks.description: The geometric language of modern physics, continuum mechanics, and geometric methods in statistics and machine learning. Manifolds, tensors, and differential forms provide the coordinate-free framework for models on curved or constrained domains.

chapters.title: Smooth Manifolds
chapters.content_outline: Topological manifolds. Charts, atlases, smooth structure. Examples: spheres, tori, Lie groups, projective spaces. Smooth maps between manifolds. Diffeomorphisms. Submanifolds and implicit function theorem on manifolds. Partition of unity.

chapters.title: Tangent Spaces and Vector Fields
chapters.content_outline: Tangent vectors as derivations. Tangent bundle TM. Vector fields as sections of TM. Integral curves and flows. Lie bracket of vector fields. Push-forward of tangent vectors. Applications: velocity fields in fluid dynamics, phase space in mechanics.

chapters.title: Covectors, Tensors, and the Cotangent Bundle
chapters.content_outline: Cotangent space and covectors (1-forms). Cotangent bundle T*M. Tensors of type (r,s): multilinear maps. Tensor products and tensor algebra. Index notation and abstract index notation. Metric tensor as a (0,2) tensor. Musical isomorphisms (raising and lowering indices). Stress and strain as tensor fields in continuum mechanics.

chapters.title: Differential Forms and Exterior Calculus
chapters.content_outline: Exterior algebra and wedge product. Differential k-forms. Exterior derivative d. Closed and exact forms. Pull-back of forms. Integration of forms on manifolds. Oriented manifolds and volume forms. Applications: conservation laws as exact forms, Maxwell's equations in differential form notation.

chapters.title: Stokes' Theorem and Its Applications
chapters.content_outline: Generalized Stokes' theorem: ∫_M dω = ∫_∂M ω. Classical theorems as special cases: fundamental theorem of calculus, Green's theorem, divergence theorem, classical Stokes' theorem. De Rham cohomology. Applications: conservation laws, circulation and flux theorems in fluid dynamics, topological invariants.

chapters.title: Riemannian Geometry
chapters.content_outline: Riemannian metrics. Length of curves and geodesics. Levi-Civita connection. Parallel transport. Curvature tensor: Riemann, Ricci, scalar curvature. Geodesic deviation. Gauss and Codazzi equations for surfaces. Applications: geometry of deformed bodies in continuum mechanics, information geometry (Fisher metric on statistical manifolds).

chapters.title: Lie Groups and Lie Algebras
chapters.content_outline: Lie groups: definition, examples (GL(n), SO(n), SU(n), SE(3)). Lie algebra as the tangent space at identity. Exponential map. Baker-Campbell-Hausdorff formula. Adjoint representation. One-parameter subgroups. Applications: rigid body dynamics (SO(3)), robotics and biomechanics (SE(3)), symmetry groups of differential equations, equivariant neural networks.

chapters.title: Applications: Continuum Mechanics in Geometric Language
chapters.content_outline: Configuration space as a manifold. Deformation as a map between manifolds. Pullback metric and strain. Stress as a section of a tensor bundle. Conservation laws via Noether's theorem. Material symmetry groups. Geometric formulation of elasticity, fluid mechanics, and electromagnetism. Discrete exterior calculus for numerical simulation.

textbooks.title: Partial Differential Equations II: Nonlinear PDEs and Modern Methods
textbooks.description: Nonlinear PDEs, conservation laws, reaction-diffusion systems, and the modern theory of existence and regularity. The mathematical tools for modeling shock waves, pattern formation, turbulence, and complex dynamics.

chapters.title: Scalar Conservation Laws and Shock Waves
chapters.content_outline: Burgers equation: characteristics, gradient catastrophe, shock formation. Weak solutions: non-uniqueness. Rankine-Hugoniot jump conditions. Entropy conditions: Lax, Oleinik, Kruzkov. Riemann problem. Rarefaction waves. Traffic flow as a conservation law. Numerical methods: Godunov, Lax-Friedrichs, and modern high-resolution schemes.

chapters.title: Systems of Conservation Laws
chapters.content_outline: Euler equations of gas dynamics. Hyperbolicity: eigenvalues of the flux Jacobian. Riemann problem for systems: shock waves, contact discontinuities, rarefaction waves. Lax's entropy condition for systems. Glimm's scheme and existence. Approximate Riemann solvers: Roe, HLL, HLLC. Applications: compressible flow, shallow water equations, magnetohydrodynamics.

chapters.title: Reaction-Diffusion Systems
chapters.content_outline: Fisher-KPP equation: traveling wave solutions, minimum wave speed. Bistable reaction-diffusion: traveling fronts. Turing instability: diffusion-driven instability, pattern formation. Activator-inhibitor systems (Gierer-Meinhardt). Spiral waves and excitable media. Applications: morphogenesis, chemical patterns, neural field equations, ecological spatial dynamics.

chapters.title: Hamilton-Jacobi Equations and Viscosity Solutions
chapters.content_outline: Hamilton-Jacobi equations: eikonal equation, optimal control connection. Classical solutions and breakdown. Viscosity solutions: definition, uniqueness, comparison principle. Existence via Perron's method. Level set methods: tracking interfaces, fast marching. Applications: optimal control, front propagation, computational geometry, image processing.

chapters.title: Navier-Stokes Equations
chapters.content_outline: Derivation from conservation of mass and momentum. Weak solutions: Leray-Hopf solutions. Energy inequality. Existence in 2D (global) and 3D (local). Regularity problem in 3D: the millennium prize problem. Serrin's regularity criterion. Numerical approaches: pressure-velocity coupling (SIMPLE, projection methods), turbulence modeling (RANS, LES).

chapters.title: Free Boundary Problems
chapters.content_outline: Stefan problem: melting and solidification. Mushy regions. Enthalpy formulation. Hele-Shaw flow. Obstacle problem as a variational inequality. American option pricing as a free boundary problem. Level set and phase field methods for moving interfaces. Applications: ice sheet dynamics, tumor growth, solidification in materials science.

chapters.title: Stochastic PDEs
chapters.content_outline: White noise and cylindrical Brownian motion. Stochastic heat equation: mild solutions. Regularity of SPDE solutions. Kardar-Parisi-Zhang (KPZ) equation. Stochastic Navier-Stokes. Numerical methods for SPDEs. Connection to spatial statistics: SPDE approach to Gaussian random fields (Lindgren, Rue, Lindström). Applications: turbulence, spatial statistics, random media.

chapters.title: Homogenization and Multiscale PDEs
chapters.content_outline: Periodic homogenization: two-scale expansion, cell problems, effective coefficients. H-convergence and G-convergence. Corrector results. Homogenization of Stokes flow in porous media (Darcy's law derivation). Homogenization of elastic composites. Computational homogenization: FE² method. Connection to multiscale methods in Part IV.

textbooks.title: Computational Statistics and Monte Carlo Methods
textbooks.description: The computational methods that make modern statistics possible. Monte Carlo, bootstrap, EM algorithm, and the theory behind simulation-based inference.

chapters.title: Random Number Generation and Simulation
chapters.content_outline: Pseudorandom number generators: linear congruential, Mersenne Twister, xoshiro. Quasi-random sequences: Halton, Sobol. Inverse transform method. Accept-reject sampling. Generating from standard distributions. Multivariate normal sampling: Cholesky factorization. Copulas for dependent random vectors.

chapters.title: Monte Carlo Integration
chapters.content_outline: Basic Monte Carlo estimator: unbiasedness, variance, CLT. Variance reduction: antithetic variates, control variates, stratified sampling. Importance sampling: optimal proposal, degeneracy in high dimensions. Rao-Blackwellization. Multi-level Monte Carlo. Computational complexity comparison with deterministic quadrature.

chapters.title: Bootstrap Methods
chapters.content_outline: Nonparametric bootstrap: Efron's original method. Bootstrap confidence intervals: percentile, basic, studentized, BCa. Theoretical justification: Edgeworth expansion. Parametric bootstrap. Double bootstrap. Bootstrap for dependent data: block bootstrap, stationary bootstrap. Subsampling. Bootstrap for complex statistics: eigenvalues, quantiles, regression.

chapters.title: Permutation Tests and Exact Methods
chapters.content_outline: Permutation testing principle. Two-sample tests, paired tests, correlation tests. Exact computation vs. Monte Carlo approximation. Multiple testing with permutations: maxT procedure, step-down minP. Conditional inference. Exact tests for 2×2 tables (Fisher's exact test). Connection to conformal prediction.

chapters.title: The EM Algorithm and Extensions
chapters.content_outline: Expectation-Maximization: derivation from Jensen's inequality, convergence theory. Examples: Gaussian mixtures, missing data, hidden Markov models. Variants: ECM, ECME, AECM, stochastic EM, Monte Carlo EM. Variational EM. Connection to variational inference. Applications: mixture models in spatial statistics, latent variable models in epidemiology.

chapters.title: Sequential Monte Carlo (Particle Filters)
chapters.content_outline: State-space models and filtering. Sequential importance sampling. Resampling: multinomial, stratified, systematic, residual. Bootstrap particle filter (Gordon et al.). Auxiliary particle filter. Particle MCMC: PMMH, SMC². Convergence theory. Applications: tracking, signal processing, online Bayesian inference, data assimilation for weather prediction.

chapters.title: Cross-Validation and Predictive Assessment
chapters.content_outline: Leave-one-out cross-validation: theory, connection to AIC (Stone). K-fold cross-validation: variance analysis, choice of K. Generalized cross-validation for linear smoothers. LOOCV via influence functions. Pareto-smoothed importance sampling LOO (PSIS-LOO) for Bayesian models. Repeated cross-validation. Nested cross-validation for hyperparameter tuning.

chapters.title: Optimization Algorithms for Statistics
chapters.content_outline: Newton-Raphson and Fisher scoring. Quasi-Newton methods: BFGS, L-BFGS. Coordinate descent for LASSO and elastic net. Proximal gradient methods. Stochastic gradient descent: convergence theory, learning rate schedules, Adam optimizer. Natural gradient descent: connection to information geometry. Expectation propagation. Applications across all statistical models.

textbooks.title: Optimization Theory for Modelers
textbooks.description: Optimization is the mathematical engine of model calibration, control, and machine learning. This course covers convex and nonconvex theory, variational methods, and optimal control.

chapters.title: Convex Sets and Convex Functions
chapters.content_outline: Convex sets: halfspaces, polyhedra, cones, ellipsoids. Separation theorems. Convex functions: characterizations, Jensen's inequality, preservation under operations. Conjugate functions (Fenchel conjugate). Sublevel sets. Epigraph. Convex hulls and extreme points. Applications: constraint sets in optimization, convex relaxation.

chapters.title: Optimality Conditions
chapters.content_outline: Unconstrained optimization: first and second-order necessary and sufficient conditions. Constrained optimization: KKT conditions. Constraint qualification: Slater, LICQ, MFCQ. Duality: Lagrangian dual, weak and strong duality, complementary slackness. Saddle point characterization. Sensitivity analysis via dual variables.

chapters.title: Convex Optimization Algorithms
chapters.content_outline: Gradient descent: convergence rates for smooth and strongly convex objectives. Projected gradient descent. Proximal operators and proximal gradient method (ISTA, FISTA). ADMM (alternating direction method of multipliers). Interior point methods: barrier functions, path following. Cutting plane methods. Complexity: polynomial time solvability of convex programs.

chapters.title: Nonconvex Optimization
chapters.content_outline: Local vs. global optima. Gradient descent on nonconvex landscapes: convergence to stationary points. Saddle points and the prevalence of saddle points in high dimensions. Stochastic gradient descent: convergence theory, variance reduction (SVRG, SAGA). Global optimization: simulated annealing, genetic algorithms, Bayesian optimization. Landscape analysis of neural network loss surfaces.

chapters.title: Calculus of Variations
chapters.content_outline: Functionals and the Euler-Lagrange equation. Boundary conditions: natural and essential. Second variation and Jacobi accessory equation. Constrained variational problems: isoperimetric problems, Lagrange multipliers in function spaces. Brachistochrone and geodesics. Direct methods: existence of minimizers via lower semicontinuity and compactness. Gamma-convergence.

chapters.title: Optimal Control Theory
chapters.content_outline: Formulation: state equations, cost functional, control constraints. Pontryagin maximum principle: adjoint equations, Hamiltonian, transversality conditions. Dynamic programming: Hamilton-Jacobi-Bellman equation, value function. Linear-quadratic regulator (LQR). Numerical methods: direct (collocation) and indirect (shooting). Applications: optimal drug dosing, disease intervention strategies, trajectory optimization.

chapters.title: Optimization on Manifolds
chapters.content_outline: Riemannian gradient and Riemannian Hessian. Retractions and vector transport. Riemannian gradient descent: convergence theory. Applications: optimization over orthogonal matrices (Stiefel manifold), positive definite matrices, low-rank matrices. Geometric optimization in machine learning. Tools: Manopt, Manifolds.jl.

chapters.title: Bilevel Optimization and Learning to Optimize
chapters.content_outline: Bilevel optimization: upper and lower level problems. Implicit differentiation for bilevel optimization. Hyperparameter optimization as bilevel optimization. Meta-learning. Learning rate schedules as optimization. Differentiable optimization layers in neural networks. Applications: SciML (learning physics parameters while training neural networks), causal discovery, automated machine learning.

textbooks.title: Generalized Linear Models and Extensions
textbooks.description: The workhorse models of applied biostatistics: regression models for non-normal response data with rigorous theory of estimation, inference, and model checking.

chapters.title: Exponential Dispersion Family and Link Functions
chapters.content_outline: Exponential family in canonical form. Natural parameter, cumulant function. Mean and variance as functions of the natural parameter. Dispersion parameter. Link functions: canonical, identity, log, logit, probit, complementary log-log. Systematic component and linear predictor.

chapters.title: Maximum Likelihood Estimation for GLMs
chapters.content_outline: Score equations. Fisher scoring algorithm (iteratively reweighted least squares). Existence and uniqueness of MLE. Asymptotic properties: consistency, asymptotic normality. Information matrix and standard errors. Wald, likelihood ratio, and score tests for GLMs. Deviance and model comparison.

chapters.title: Logistic Regression
chapters.content_outline: Binary logistic regression: interpretation of coefficients as log-odds ratios. Multinomial and ordinal logistic regression. Separation and quasi-complete separation. Firth's penalized likelihood. ROC curves and AUC. Calibration assessment. Hosmer-Lemeshow test. Conditional and exact logistic regression.

chapters.title: Poisson Regression and Count Data Models
chapters.content_outline: Poisson regression: log link, interpretation as rate ratios. Offset term for exposure. Overdispersion: quasi-Poisson, negative binomial regression. Zero-inflated models: ZIP, ZINB. Hurdle models. Rate modeling with person-time denominators. Applications: disease incidence modeling, event counts in clinical trials.

chapters.title: Quasi-Likelihood and Generalized Estimating Equations
chapters.content_outline: Quasi-likelihood: relaxing distributional assumptions, keeping mean-variance relationship. Quasi-AIC. Generalized estimating equations (GEE) for correlated data: working correlation structures (independence, exchangeable, AR(1), unstructured). Sandwich (robust) standard errors. QIC for working correlation selection. Comparison with mixed models.

chapters.title: Penalized Regression: Ridge, LASSO, Elastic Net
chapters.content_outline: Ridge regression: L2 penalty, bias-variance tradeoff, connection to Bayesian normal prior. LASSO: L1 penalty, sparsity, geometric interpretation. Elastic net: combining L1 and L2. Coordinate descent algorithm. Cross-validation for tuning parameter selection. Adaptive LASSO for oracle properties. Group LASSO. Stability selection. Applications: variable selection in high-dimensional clinical data.

chapters.title: Generalized Additive Models
chapters.content_outline: Smoothing splines: penalized regression splines, thin plate splines. Tensor product smooths for interactions. Backfitting algorithm. Penalized iteratively reweighted least squares. Automatic smoothness selection: GCV, REML. Concurvity. GAMs in mgcv (R) and GAM.jl. Applications: dose-response curves, nonlinear exposure-outcome relationships.

chapters.title: Diagnostics, Residuals, and Model Checking
chapters.content_outline: Deviance residuals, Pearson residuals, Anscombe residuals. Leverage and influence in GLMs. Cook's distance. Added variable plots. Partial residual plots. Goodness-of-fit tests. Overdispersion testing. Residual diagnostics for GAMs. Posterior predictive checking for Bayesian GLMs. Simulation-based residuals (DHARMa approach).

textbooks.title: Survival Analysis: Theory and Advanced Methods
textbooks.description: The mathematics of time-to-event data: censoring, hazard functions, and the counting process framework that unifies nonparametric, semiparametric, and parametric approaches.

chapters.title: Survival Functions, Hazards, and Censoring
chapters.content_outline: Survival function, hazard function, cumulative hazard, and their relationships. Types of censoring: right, left, interval. Truncation: left and right. Likelihood construction under censoring. Common parametric distributions: exponential, Weibull, log-normal, log-logistic, Gompertz. Mean residual life. Conditional survival.

chapters.title: Nonparametric Estimation
chapters.content_outline: Kaplan-Meier estimator: derivation, Greenwood's formula for variance. Nelson-Aalen estimator. Log-rank test and its extensions (weighted log-rank, stratified log-rank). Renyi-type tests. Confidence bands: Hall-Wellner, EP. Median survival estimation. Multiple comparison adjustments.

chapters.title: Cox Proportional Hazards Model
chapters.content_outline: Semiparametric model: no baseline hazard specification. Partial likelihood: derivation, properties, asymptotic theory (Andersen-Gill). Breslow and Efron approximations for ties. Time-varying covariates. Stratified Cox model. Baseline hazard estimation: Breslow, Nelson-Aalen. Interpretation of hazard ratios.

chapters.title: Counting Process Formulation
chapters.content_outline: Counting processes N(t), at-risk processes Y(t). Martingale residuals. Nelson-Aalen via counting processes. Multiplicative intensity model. Martingale CLT. Andersen-Gill extension for recurrent events. Aalen's additive hazards model. Connection to empirical process theory. This formulation provides the rigorous mathematical framework for all survival analysis.

chapters.title: Accelerated Failure Time Models
chapters.content_outline: AFT model: log-linear form, interpretation as time acceleration/deceleration. Parametric AFT: Weibull, log-normal, log-logistic. Semiparametric AFT: rank-based estimation (Gehan, logrank). Comparison with Cox PH model. When to use AFT vs. PH. Bayesian AFT models.

chapters.title: Competing Risks
chapters.content_outline: Competing risks framework: cause-specific hazards, subdistribution hazards. Cause-specific Cox models. Fine-Gray subdistribution hazard model. Cumulative incidence function: Aalen-Johansen estimator. Gray's test. Multi-state models: illness-death model, general multi-state. Transition probabilities and hazards. Aalen-Johansen estimator for multi-state models.

chapters.title: Frailty Models and Correlated Survival
chapters.content_outline: Shared frailty models: gamma, log-normal, inverse Gaussian frailty distributions. Penalized partial likelihood estimation. Connection to random effects models. Correlated frailty. Multi-center clinical trials with frailty. Cure fraction models: mixture cure model, promotion time cure model. Applications: family studies, multi-center trials, dental survival.

chapters.title: Joint Models for Longitudinal and Survival Data
chapters.content_outline: Shared parameter joint models. Bayesian estimation via MCMC. Dynamic predictions: updating survival probabilities as longitudinal data accumulates. Discrimination and calibration of dynamic predictions. Software: JMbayes2, rstanarm. Applications: biomarker-driven survival prediction, personalized medicine, oncology dose optimization.

textbooks.title: Neural Differential Equations and Universal Differential Equations
textbooks.description: The core course of the SciML track: using neural networks inside differential equations to create hybrid mechanistic-data-driven models. Covers neural ODEs, physics-informed neural networks, and the universal differential equation framework.

chapters.title: Neural ODEs: Continuous-Depth Neural Networks
chapters.content_outline: ResNets as Euler discretizations of ODEs. Neural ODE formulation: dx/dt = f_θ(x,t). Forward pass via ODE solver. Memory-efficient training via adjoint sensitivity method (continuous adjoint). Comparison with backpropagation through the solver (discrete adjoint). Regularization: weight decay, Jacobian penalty, augmented neural ODEs. Computational considerations: adaptive stepping, implicit solvers for stiff neural ODEs.

chapters.title: Training Neural ODEs: Sensitivity Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Forward sensitivity analysis: O(np) cost. Adjoint sensitivity analysis: O(n) cost independent of parameters. Continuous vs. discrete adjoint: accuracy tradeoffs. Interpolating adjoint for memory efficiency. Automatic differentiation through ODE solvers. Checkpointing strategies. Sensitivity analysis in DiffEqFlux.jl and torchdiffeq.

chapters.title: Universal Differential Equations (UDEs)
chapters.content_outline: The UDE paradigm: known physics + neural network for unknown terms. Formulation: dx/dt = f_known(x) + NN_θ(x). Training: adjoint methods through the hybrid system. Symbolic regression for interpretability: recovering equations from trained neural networks (SINDy post-processing). Identifiability of neural network terms. Regularization to promote physical plausibility. Examples: learning missing physics in Lotka-Volterra, Lorenz, pharmacokinetics.

chapters.title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks (PINNs)
chapters.content_outline: Formulation: neural network as approximate solution, PDE residual as loss. Collocation points and residual sampling strategies. Boundary and initial condition enforcement: soft (penalty) vs. hard (architecture). Training challenges: spectral bias, causality failure, multiscale problems. Remedies: adaptive weighting, curriculum learning, Fourier feature embeddings, causal training. Convergence theory: what do PINNs approximate and when do they fail?

chapters.title: Neural Operators: DeepONet and Fourier Neural Operator
chapters.content_outline: Learning operators (maps between function spaces) rather than functions. DeepONet: branch and trunk network architecture, universal approximation for operators. Fourier Neural Operator (FNO): spectral convolution layers, resolution invariance. U-shaped Neural Operator (UNO). Comparison with PINNs: operator learning generalizes across initial/boundary conditions. Training data requirements. Applications: weather prediction (FourCastNet), fluid dynamics, material science.

chapters.title: Neural SDEs and Stochastic Models
chapters.content_outline: Neural SDE formulation: drift and diffusion as neural networks. Training via adjoint methods for SDEs. Latent SDE models for irregularly sampled time series. Score-based diffusion models as neural SDEs. Variational inference for neural SDEs. Applications: stochastic population dynamics, financial modeling, uncertainty quantification in dynamical systems.

chapters.title: Structure-Preserving Neural Networks
chapters.content_outline: Hamiltonian neural networks: learning energy-conserving dynamics. Lagrangian neural networks. Symplectic neural networks. Port-Hamiltonian neural networks for dissipative systems. SE(3)-equivariant networks for molecular dynamics. Conservation law enforcement: divergence-free networks for incompressible flow, positivity constraints. Noether's theorem as architectural constraint.

chapters.title: Surrogate Modeling and Emulation
chapters.content_outline: Neural network surrogates for expensive simulations. Training data generation: Latin hypercube sampling, space-filling designs. Active learning for efficient data acquisition. Multi-fidelity surrogates: combining cheap low-fidelity and expensive high-fidelity simulations. Transfer learning for surrogate models. Uncertainty quantification for surrogates: ensemble methods, MC dropout, conformal prediction. Applications: climate model emulation, patient-specific biomechanical simulation, real-time digital twins.

chapters.title: Inverse Problems and System Identification with SciML
chapters.content_outline: Classical inverse problems: ill-posedness, regularization. SciML approach: embed the forward model (ODE/PDE) as a differentiable layer, optimize parameters via gradient descent through the solver. Hybrid inverse problems: learn both parameters and missing physics simultaneously. Bayesian SciML: posterior inference over neural network weights and physical parameters. Amortized inference with neural posterior estimation. Applications: estimating cardiovascular parameters from pressure waveforms, identifying ecological interaction networks from time-series data.

chapters.title: Case Studies: SciML Across Domains
chapters.content_outline: Case Study 1: Neonatal hemodynamics — UDE with Windkessel equations and neural network for unknown compliance.
