Case Study 2: Epidemic forecasting — neural ODE augmenting SIR model with learned behavioral response. Case Study 3: Climate emulation — FNO trained on CESM output for fast scenario evaluation. Case Study 4: Biomechanics — PINN for tissue mechanics with learned constitutive law. Case Study 5: Pharmacokinetics — neural SDE for patient-specific drug metabolism. Each case includes data, code, and validation.

textbooks.title: Continuum Mechanics: From Fundamentals to Biological Tissue
textbooks.description: The mathematical theory of continuous media: solids, fluids, and biological tissues. Provides the unified framework for biomechanics, fluid dynamics, and any model involving deformable bodies.

chapters.title: Kinematics of Deformation
chapters.content_outline: Body, configuration, motion. Material (Lagrangian) and spatial (Eulerian) descriptions. Deformation gradient F. Polar decomposition: rotation and stretch. Strain measures: Green-Lagrange strain E, Almansi strain e, logarithmic strain. Rate of deformation tensor D, spin tensor W. Material time derivative. Reynolds transport theorem.

chapters.title: Stress and Balance Laws
chapters.content_outline: Cauchy stress tensor: traction vector, Cauchy's theorem. First and second Piola-Kirchhoff stress tensors. Balance of mass (continuity equation). Balance of linear momentum (equations of motion). Balance of angular momentum (symmetry of Cauchy stress). Balance of energy (first law of thermodynamics). Entropy inequality (second law, Clausius-Duhem).

chapters.title: Constitutive Theory: Principles and Constraints
chapters.content_outline: Constitutive equations: mapping deformation to stress. Principles of constitutive theory: determinism, local action, material frame indifference (objectivity), material symmetry. Representation theorems for isotropic tensor functions. Thermodynamic restrictions from Clausius-Duhem inequality. Coleman-Noll procedure. Simple materials. Internal variables.

chapters.title: Linear Elasticity
chapters.content_outline: Linearization of finite elasticity. Generalized Hooke's law: stiffness tensor, compliance tensor. Symmetry classes: isotropic (2 constants: λ, μ), transversely isotropic (5), orthotropic (9), anisotropic (21). Navier equations. Plane stress and plane strain. Airy stress function. Saint-Venant's principle. Variational formulation and finite element methods for linear elasticity.

chapters.title: Hyperelasticity
chapters.content_outline: Strain energy functions: W(F) or W(C) or W(E). Stress from energy: S = ∂W/∂E, P = ∂W/∂F. Neo-Hookean model. Mooney-Rivlin model. Ogden model. Arruda-Boyce (eight-chain) model. Nearly incompressible formulations: penalty and mixed methods. Polyconvexity and existence of minimizers. Material stability (strong ellipticity). Implementation in FEM.

chapters.title: Viscoelasticity
chapters.content_outline: Linear viscoelasticity: creep and relaxation functions, Boltzmann superposition. Spring-dashpot models: Maxwell, Kelvin-Voigt, standard linear solid, generalized Maxwell (Prony series). Complex modulus and loss tangent. Correspondence principle. Nonlinear viscoelasticity: quasi-linear viscoelasticity (Fung model for biological tissue). Finite deformation viscoelasticity: multiplicative decomposition of deformation gradient.

chapters.title: Biological Tissue Mechanics
chapters.content_outline: Special properties of biological tissue: large deformations, anisotropy (fiber-reinforced), viscoelasticity, incompressibility, active contraction, growth and remodeling. Holzapfel-Gasser-Ogden (HGO) model for arterial tissue. Fiber dispersion. Residual stress and opening angle experiments. Active stress vs. active strain formulations for muscle. Mixture theory for soft tissue: solid skeleton + fluid.

chapters.title: Growth and Remodeling
chapters.content_outline: Volumetric growth: multiplicative decomposition F = F_e · F_g. Growth laws: stress-driven, strain-driven, nutrient-driven. Morphoelasticity: mechanics of growing biological structures. Tumor growth models. Wound healing mechanics. Bone remodeling: Wolff's law, computational models. Cardiac growth and remodeling in hypertrophy. Connection to agent-based models of tissue growth.

chapters.title: Poroelasticity and Mixture Theory
chapters.content_outline: Biot's theory of poroelasticity: coupled solid deformation and fluid flow. Effective stress. Consolidation theory (Terzaghi). Darcy's law from homogenization. Anisotropic poroelasticity. Mixture theory: balance laws for multi-component continua. Biphasic and triphasic models for cartilage. Swelling of hydrogels. Applications: brain tissue (poroelastic model of hydrocephalus), intervertebral disc, lung tissue.

chapters.title: Computational Continuum Mechanics
chapters.content_outline: Weak form and virtual work principle. Finite element discretization: isoparametric elements, Gauss quadrature. Nonlinear FEM: Newton-Raphson iteration, consistent tangent stiffness. Large deformation FEM: total Lagrangian and updated Lagrangian. Mixed formulations for incompressibility. Contact mechanics. Adaptive mesh refinement. Verification and validation for biomechanical FEM. Software: FEBio, FEniCS, Ferrite.jl.

textbooks.title: Geophysical Fluid Dynamics and Atmospheric Modeling
textbooks.description: The dynamics of rotating, stratified fluids on planetary scales: the mathematical foundation for weather prediction, ocean modeling, and climate science.

chapters.title: Governing Equations on a Rotating Earth
chapters.content_outline: Navier-Stokes in a rotating frame: Coriolis and centrifugal accelerations. Primitive equations: hydrostatic approximation. Pressure coordinates. Thermodynamic energy equation. Equation of state for moist air. Boussinesq approximation. Shallow water equations: derivation, conservation properties. Spherical coordinates and the beta-plane approximation.

chapters.title: Geostrophic Balance and Thermal Wind
chapters.content_outline: Geostrophic balance: Coriolis force balancing pressure gradient. Geostrophic wind. Thermal wind relation: vertical wind shear from horizontal temperature gradients. Ageostrophic motions. Ekman layers: boundary layer in rotating fluid, Ekman spiral, Ekman pumping. Applications: large-scale atmospheric and oceanic circulation patterns.

chapters.title: Rossby Waves and Planetary Wave Dynamics
chapters.content_outline: Potential vorticity: Ertel's theorem, conservation on isentropic surfaces. Rossby waves: barotropic and baroclinic. Dispersion relation and group velocity. Rossby wave breaking. Wave-mean flow interaction: Eliassen-Palm flux. Critical layers. Vertically propagating waves. Stationary waves forced by topography and heating.

chapters.title: Baroclinic Instability
chapters.content_outline: Eady model: growth rates, most unstable mode, structure. Charney model: semi-infinite atmosphere. Necessary conditions for instability: Charney-Stern theorem. Energetics: conversion of available potential energy to kinetic energy. Life cycle of baroclinic waves. Connection to mid-latitude weather systems (cyclogenesis).

chapters.title: Quasi-Geostrophic Theory
chapters.content_outline: QG scaling and asymptotic derivation from primitive equations. QG potential vorticity equation. QG omega equation: diagnosis of vertical motion. QG energetics. Numerical solution of the QG system. Limitations and when QG breaks down. Connection to more complete models.

chapters.title: Parameterization of Sub-Grid Processes
chapters.content_outline: Why parameterization is necessary: processes below grid resolution. Convective parameterization: mass flux schemes, CAPE-based triggers. Boundary layer parameterization: K-theory, TKE closures. Radiation: two-stream approximation, correlated-k method. Cloud microphysics: bulk vs. bin approaches. Stochastic parameterization. Machine learning parameterization: neural network emulators of sub-grid processes.

chapters.title: Numerical Methods for Atmospheric Models
chapters.content_outline: Spectral transform method: spherical harmonics, transform grid. Finite volume on the cubed-sphere grid. Discontinuous Galerkin methods for atmospheric dynamics. Time integration: semi-implicit, split-explicit. Advection schemes: flux-form semi-Lagrangian. Conservation properties: mass, energy, potential vorticity. Grid imprinting and computational mode issues.

chapters.title: Data Assimilation for Weather Prediction
chapters.content_outline: Optimal interpolation. 3D-Var and 4D-Var: cost function formulation, adjoint model, incremental approach. Ensemble Kalman filter: stochastic and deterministic variants, localization, inflation. Hybrid methods: combining ensemble and variational. Observation operators: satellite radiances, radar, radiosondes. Quality control. Forecast verification: skill scores, reliability diagrams, economic value.

chapters.title: Climate Modeling and Earth System Science
chapters.content_outline: Energy balance models: zero and one-dimensional. Radiative-convective equilibrium. Climate sensitivity and feedback analysis: Planck, water vapor, lapse rate, albedo, cloud feedbacks. Carbon cycle: terrestrial and ocean. Coupled ocean-atmosphere models: AMOC, ENSO. Ice sheet dynamics: shallow ice approximation. Climate model evaluation. Detection and attribution of climate change.

chapters.title: Climate Model Emulation and SciML in Climate Science
chapters.content_outline: Gaussian process emulators for climate model output. Neural network emulators: training on CMIP ensembles. Fourier Neural Operator for weather prediction (Pangu-Weather, GraphCast). Hybrid models: ML-corrected climate models. Uncertainty quantification: perturbed physics ensembles. Integrated assessment models. Scenario analysis: SSP-RCP framework. Ethical considerations in climate modeling.

textbooks.title: Uncertainty Quantification: Theory and Practice
textbooks.description: The mathematical framework for quantifying, propagating, and reducing uncertainty in computational models. UQ is the bridge between model and reality — without it, predictions are meaningless.

chapters.title: Taxonomy of Uncertainty
chapters.content_outline: Aleatory (irreducible) vs. epistemic (reducible) uncertainty. Parametric uncertainty, structural uncertainty (model form error), numerical uncertainty (discretization error). Input uncertainty, output uncertainty. Uncertainty in initial conditions, boundary conditions, forcing. The uncertainty pyramid: from data to decisions.

chapters.title: Forward Uncertainty Propagation: Monte Carlo Methods
chapters.content_outline: Standard Monte Carlo: convergence rate O(1/√N). Variance reduction: antithetic variates, control variates, importance sampling. Quasi-Monte Carlo: low-discrepancy sequences, Koksma-Hlawka inequality. Latin hypercube sampling. Multilevel Monte Carlo: variance reduction by combining cheap and expensive models. Multilevel quasi-Monte Carlo.

chapters.title: Polynomial Chaos Expansions
chapters.content_outline: Wiener-Askey scheme: matching polynomial basis to input distribution (Hermite-Gaussian, Legendre-uniform, etc.). Intrusive methods: stochastic Galerkin. Non-intrusive methods: stochastic collocation, regression. Sparse grids (Smolyak). Adaptive polynomial chaos. Convergence rates. Multi-element polynomial chaos for discontinuities. Comparison with Monte Carlo for different problem dimensions.

chapters.title: Global Sensitivity Analysis
chapters.content_outline: Variance-based methods: Sobol indices (first-order, total-order, higher-order interactions). ANOVA decomposition. Estimation of Sobol indices: Monte Carlo (Saltelli), polynomial chaos. Morris screening for factor fixing. Shapley effects for correlated inputs. Derivative-based global sensitivity measures (DGSM). Applications: identifying which parameters most influence model predictions.

chapters.title: Bayesian Calibration of Computer Models
chapters.content_outline: Kennedy-O'Hagan framework: calibration parameter θ, model inadequacy δ(x), observation error ε. Gaussian process emulator for the computer model. Identifiability: separating calibration parameters from model discrepancy. Modular vs. full Bayesian calibration. History matching: implausibility measures, iterative refocusing. Applications: calibrating climate models, biomechanical models, pharmacokinetic models.

chapters.title: Surrogate-Based UQ
chapters.content_outline: Gaussian process surrogates: prediction mean and variance. Adaptive design: expected improvement, integrated mean squared prediction error. Neural network surrogates with uncertainty: ensembles, MC dropout, Bayesian neural networks. Multi-fidelity surrogates: co-kriging, multi-fidelity neural networks. Active subspaces: dimension reduction for UQ. Applications: real-time UQ for expensive simulations.

chapters.title: Reliability Analysis and Rare Event Estimation
chapters.content_outline: Limit state functions and failure probability. First-order reliability method (FORM): most probable point, Hasofer-Lind index. Second-order reliability method (SORM). Importance sampling for rare events. Subset simulation: Markov chain sampling in nested subsets. Line sampling. Applications: structural reliability, nuclear safety, extreme weather events.

chapters.title: Model Validation and Prediction Under Uncertainty
chapters.content_outline: Verification vs. validation. Model validation metrics: area metric, statistical hypothesis tests, Bayesian validation. Validation domains and extrapolation. Predictive assessment: posterior predictive checks, calibration-sharp prediction. Decision under uncertainty: expected utility, robust optimization. Value of information analysis: how much should we invest in reducing uncertainty? Communicating uncertainty to stakeholders.

textbooks.title: Advanced Dynamical Systems: From Chaos to Data-Driven Discovery
textbooks.description: Nonlinear dynamics, chaos, and modern data-driven methods for discovering and analyzing dynamical systems from data. Connects classical dynamical systems theory to machine learning and SciML.

chapters.title: Invariant Manifolds and Global Dynamics
chapters.content_outline: Stable, unstable, and center manifolds. Stable manifold theorem. Center manifold reduction for bifurcation analysis. Invariant tori. Homoclinic and heteroclinic orbits. Melnikov method for homoclinic bifurcations. Global bifurcations: homoclinic explosion, Shilnikov. Applications: transport barriers in geophysical flows, escape from potential wells in biomechanics.

chapters.title: Chaos and Strange Attractors
chapters.content_outline: Sensitive dependence on initial conditions. Lyapunov exponents: definition, computation, spectrum. Strange attractors: Lorenz, Rössler, Hénon. Fractal dimension: box-counting, correlation dimension, Kaplan-Yorke. Horseshoe maps and symbolic dynamics. Takens embedding theorem: reconstructing attractors from time series. Chaos in Hamiltonian systems: KAM theorem.

chapters.title: Ergodic Theory for Dynamical Systems
chapters.content_outline: Invariant measures. Birkhoff ergodic theorem. Mixing and ergodicity. Ergodic hierarchy: ergodic → mixing → K-system → Bernoulli. Physical (SRB) measures. Ergodic theory of Markov processes. Connection to statistical mechanics. Applications: time averages equal ensemble averages (justification for long-time simulation statistics).

chapters.title: Koopman Operator Theory
chapters.content_outline: Koopman operator: infinite-dimensional linear representation of nonlinear dynamics. Koopman eigenfunctions, eigenvalues, and modes. Spectral decomposition of observables. Connection to harmonic analysis and ergodic theory. Koopman analysis of periodic, quasi-periodic, and chaotic systems. Applications: linear prediction of nonlinear systems, control design, model reduction.

chapters.title: Dynamic Mode Decomposition (DMD)
chapters.content_outline: DMD algorithm: data-driven approximation of Koopman operator. Standard DMD, exact DMD, optimized DMD. Connection to SVD and low-rank approximation. Extended DMD (EDMD) with dictionary functions. Kernel DMD. DMD with control. Sparsity-promoting DMD. Applications: fluid dynamics (coherent structure identification), neuroscience, epidemiology (identifying dominant modes in disease dynamics).

chapters.title: Sparse Identification of Nonlinear Dynamics (SINDy)
chapters.content_outline: SINDy algorithm: sparse regression on a library of candidate functions. Sequential thresholded least squares (STLSQ). Choosing the library: polynomial, trigonometric, custom. Handling noise: integral formulation, ensemble SINDy. SINDy with control inputs. PDE-FIND: discovering PDEs from data. Implicit SINDy for rational dynamics. Bayesian SINDy for uncertainty quantification. Applications: discovering governing equations for unknown systems.

chapters.title: Data-Driven Reduced-Order Models
chapters.content_outline: Proper Orthogonal Decomposition (POD): SVD of snapshot matrix, Galerkin projection. Balanced truncation. POD-Galerkin reduced models: limitations (stability, accuracy). Closure models for reduced systems. Neural network closure. Autoencoders for nonlinear dimension reduction. Graph-based reduction for network dynamics. Applications: real-time simulation of parameterized PDEs, digital twins.

chapters.title: Control of Dynamical Systems
chapters.content_outline: Controllability and observability. State feedback: pole placement, LQR. Output feedback: observer design, separation principle. Nonlinear control: feedback linearization, sliding mode control. Model predictive control (MPC). Data-driven control: reinforcement learning for continuous systems. Koopman-based control. Applications: controlling epidemics (optimal intervention timing), bioreactor control, prosthetics.

textbooks.title: Optimal Transport: Theory, Computation, and Applications Across Domains
textbooks.description: Optimal transport provides a geometry on the space of probability distributions. This emerging field connects analysis, geometry, probability, and computation with applications in every domain of this curriculum.

chapters.title: Monge and Kantorovich Formulations
chapters.content_outline: Monge's problem: finding the optimal map. Kantorovich relaxation: transport plans. Existence of optimal plans. Wasserstein distances: W1 (earth mover's distance), W2. Kantorovich duality. c-concavity and c-transforms. Metric properties of Wasserstein spaces.

chapters.title: Brenier's Theorem and Structure of Optimal Maps
chapters.content_outline: Brenier's theorem: optimal map is the gradient of a convex function. Monge-Ampère equation. Regularity of optimal maps: Caffarelli's regularity theory. McCann's displacement interpolation. Geodesics in Wasserstein space. Comparison with Fisher-Rao distance on statistical manifolds.

chapters.title: Entropic Regularization and the Sinkhorn Algorithm
chapters.content_outline: Entropic optimal transport. Sinkhorn-Knopp algorithm: matrix scaling, linear convergence. Computational complexity. Debiased Sinkhorn divergences. Connection to Schrödinger bridges. GPU-accelerated implementations. Applications: large-scale distribution comparison, image matching, natural language processing.

chapters.title: Gradient Flows in Wasserstein Space
chapters.content_outline: Jordan-Kinderlehrer-Otto (JKO) scheme: implicit Euler in Wasserstein space. Heat equation as gradient flow of entropy. Fokker-Planck equation as gradient flow of free energy. Porous medium equation. Applications: diffusion processes, Bayesian inference as gradient flow (Stein variational gradient descent), sampling algorithms.

chapters.title: Computational Optimal Transport
chapters.content_outline: Discrete optimal transport: linear programming. Network simplex algorithm. Auction algorithm. Continuous optimal transport: Benamou-Brenier formulation (fluid mechanics). Semi-discrete optimal transport. Wasserstein barycenters: Fréchet mean of distributions. Free support barycenters. Sliced Wasserstein distance: 1D projections for fast computation.

chapters.title: Applications in Machine Learning and Statistics
chapters.content_outline: Wasserstein GANs: using Wasserstein distance as training objective. Distributional regression: predicting entire distributions. Domain adaptation via optimal transport. Fairness: removing distributional bias. Wasserstein distances for hypothesis testing. Two-sample tests based on optimal transport. Optimal transport for causal inference.

chapters.title: Applications in Natural Sciences
chapters.content_outline: Computational biology: single-cell trajectory inference (Waddington-OT), cell fate prediction. Geophysics: moisture transport in the atmosphere. Ecology: comparing species distributions. Economics: matching markets, assignment problems. Fluid dynamics: semi-geostrophic equations as optimal transport. Cancer biology: tracking tumor evolution.

chapters.title: Unbalanced Optimal Transport and Extensions
chapters.content_outline: Unbalanced OT: relaxing mass conservation. Wasserstein-Fisher-Rao (Hellinger-Kantorovich) distance. Gromov-Wasserstein distance: comparing distributions on different spaces. Partial optimal transport. Multi-marginal optimal transport: Wasserstein barycenters as a special case. Martingale optimal transport: finance applications. Quantum optimal transport.
